page: Amazon Web Services m8a.24xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPUs. Network connectivity is supported by a 40 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results place the m8a.24xlarge in the top tier (top 10%) across almost all evaluated categories. The dedicated AMD EPYC cores deliver strong single-core and multi-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests, including software compilation, ray tracing, and cryptographic operations. Memory performance is highly competitive, with DDR5 memory bandwidth reaching top-tier speeds for both cached and uncached read/write operations. In database and key-value store simulations, the server achieved top-tier results, including high Redis SET throughput and Passmark database operation scores. For language model workloads, the instance demonstrates top-tier prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models, though text generation for very small models falls into the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8a.24xlarge offers high resource density and top-tier performance per resource for general-purpose workloads, leveraging the efficiency of the AMD EPYC architecture and DDR5 memory. However, a notable tradeoff is the complete absence of local NVMe storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. Additionally, the lack of dedicated GPU hardware limits its suitability for heavy deep learning training, despite strong CPU-based LLM inference capabilities. This server is optimized for large-scale web serving, enterprise databases, software compilation pipelines, and CPU-driven data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m8a.24xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs. It features 384.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying on a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks place this server in the top tier for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. It also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in CPU-based LLM prompt processing. The lack of local storage requires network-attached storage solutions, representing a key architectural tradeoff. This server is optimized for enterprise databases, large-scale web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose compute workloads requiring high memory bandwidth and dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.24xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 40 Gbps network baseline, delivering top-tier CPU and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.24xlarge is a general-purpose server with 96 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier benchmark performance.
tagline: A high-density, general-purpose platform delivering dedicated AMD EPYC processing power and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 96-core AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 384 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Delivers top-tier single-core, multi-core, and memory bandwidth performance
Features a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for enterprise databases, web serving, and software compilation
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage
